refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control systems.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency constraints.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize network protocols and data compression to reduce latency.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes to process commands closer to vehicle.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation traffic over other data streams in network scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize network protocols and data compression to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Interaction between network protocol optimization and telemetry data format.
Dependency on network link quality and scheduling.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses latency reduction with minimal coupling to other system functions; edge computing (DP2) and traffic prioritization (DP3) introduce higher architectural complexity and coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not meet latency under degraded network conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 or DP3 if latency targets are not met in testing.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication modules responsible for teleoperation command and control, including protocol handling and data compression.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation commands and telemetry data over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time vehicle status and sensor data feedback to the operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands and task instructions for teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion risk as functionality is centralized.
Vehicle module handles execution but latency optimization logic is minimal here.
Safety services monitor but do not directly control latency.
Broad module with mixed responsibilities; less ideal for focused latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization logic and communication protocol handling are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and maximize ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link quality outside Teleoperation control.
Risk of latency-related logic spreading into Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules if not carefully managed.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Establish clear interface contracts and monitoring to prevent diffusion of latency logic outside Teleoperation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (network protocol optimization) directly addresses the refined FR with clear measurable targets and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Latency logic potentially spreading into Vehicle or NGHE Solution modules if boundaries are not enforced.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment scores and low diffusion risk, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified via telemetry and network monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and enforce interface boundaries to prevent diffusion of latency logic.
Plan for fallback or augmentation with edge computing if latency targets are not met.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirement with clear verification paths and manageable risks. No further decomposition needed at this stage.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency between operator and vehicle control systems.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link with minimal packet loss and jitter.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry feedback to operator within latency constraints.

revise.why: Current artifacts meet criteria; future revisions may be triggered by verification outcomes.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Telemetry data accurately reflects latency performance.
open_risks: Latency targets may not be met under degraded network conditions.
Potential diffusion of latency logic outside Teleoperation module.
evidence_needed: Latency measurement reports from teleoperation sessions.
Interface compliance audits to ensure containment.
